quote: Q4 2025 revenue RMB 123,912.2M (+12% YoY). FY2025 revenue RMB 431,845.7M (+10% YoY). Q4 operating profit RMB 27,719.7M (+8.3% YoY).
Supreme Court struck down IEEPA tariffs Feb 20, 2026 (Learning Resources v. Trump, 6-3). IEEPA tariffs invalidated but de minimis still dead, Section 301/232 remain.

Co-CEO Zhao: '2026 marks the beginning of our journey into the next decade.' 'Supply chain investment is where we will place our greatest conviction.' VP Finance Liu: 'These investments are firm and long-term, and will inevitably affect our financial performance.'
FY2025 operating profit RMB 94,624.1M (-13% YoY). Operating margin 21.9% (down from 27.5%). Gross margin 56.3% (down from 60.9%, -460bps). CoR +23%, S&M +13%, R&D +30%, G&A -12%.

13 self-owned Temu warehouses globally (10 EU, 2 US, 1 US-Mexico border). 14th planned Brazil. Semi-managed now 80% of US sales. US daily packages dropped ~70% to 250-300K/day. Cross-border direct-ship has NOT resumed post-SCOTUS.
FY2025 interest & investment income: RMB 25.6B (+24% YoY from RMB 20.6B). 27% of operating profit. Net income (RMB 99.4B) exceeds operating profit (RMB 94.6B) due to interest income.
Dec 31, 2025 balance sheet: Cash RMB 108.9B + ST investments RMB 313.4B + restricted cash RMB 73.8B + non-current deposits RMB 104.7B = total liquid assets RMB 600.8B (~$83.5B).
Analyst consensus: 14 analysts, Hold rating, $140.57 target (32% upside from $106.23). Consensus expects 25% earnings rebound in FY2026.
SCOTUS invalidated all IEEPA tariffs. Yale Budget Lab: effective China tariff rate cut by ~2/3 (16% to 9.1%). Section 301 (7.5-25%), Section 232, Section 122 (15% global, expires July 24) remain.
One Big Beautiful Bill Act signed July 4, 2025. Permanently repeals de minimis exemption effective July 1, 2027. De minimis was separately suspended by executive action May 2, 2025.
Net tariff on typical Temu product post-SCOTUS: ~30-45% (Section 301 + Section 122 + MFN duty). Down from ~120-145% pre-SCOTUS. $10 product: was $24.50, now ~$14.50.
Temu US daily packages dropped ~70%: from ~1M/day (2023-24) to ~250-300K/day post-de-minimis. No evidence of volume recovery post-SCOTUS (ruling 4 weeks ago).
Temu US MAU (web): collapsed 76% from ~34M to ~8M. App MAU: 133.6M (-28% YoY, Oct 2025). Temu resumed US ad spending: 1,100 Meta ads, 700 Google ads active. Re-entered App Store top ranks.
Semi-managed orders represent 80% of US sales. 70% of platform traffic directed to semi-managed SKUs. Self-operated US warehouses handle 15-20% of volume.
Temu pricing partially recovered via US warehouse fulfillment and 18% cuts on bestsellers (Sept 2025). NOT to pre-tariff levels. Post-SCOTUS: tariff burden halved but de minimis dead.
Temu EU MAU: 141.6M (+74% YoY). LatAm MAU: +122% YoY. Global MAU: 530M+ despite US decline.
EU GMV on track for $15-20B in 2025, potentially $25B+ in 2026. Europe now ~40% of Temu GMV, surpassing US (~31%).
EU agreed Dec 12, 2025: €3 duty per item on parcels under €150, effective July 1, 2026. €2/parcel handling fee follows November 2026. 4.6B retail parcels entered EU, 91% from China.
Domestic OMS take rate ~4.1% (FY2025 OMS RMB 217.8B / estimated domestic GMV ~RMB 5.3T). Approaching Alibaba's ~4.5-5%. Take rate expansion plateauing.

29-article pricing regulation (NDRC + SAMR + CAC) effective April 10, 2026. Bans forcing lowest-price agreements, using traffic throttling/algorithm penalties for predatory pricing, algorithmic price discrimination. 5-year validity through 2031.
PDD investing RMB 10B+ in instant retail (Sept 2025-Sept 2026). 200 warehouses in Shanghai. Last major CGB platform after Meituan Youxuan exit.
Duoduo Maicai GMV +340% YoY in July 2025 after Meituan Youxuan exit. Goldman projects instant commerce TAM at RMB 1.5T by 2030. Meituan has 6,000+ warehouses vs PDD's 200.
SAMR identifies '全网最低价' as one of eight monopoly risk scenarios. PDD dropped explicit lowest-price requirement during 618 2025, replacing with '同款同价' (same product, same price).
FY2025 OCF: RMB 106,938.7M ($14.9B). Owner earnings: ~RMB 98B (~$13.6B) = OCF minus capex (~RMB 1B) minus SBC (RMB 7.94B). EV/OE ~5.7x at $106.23.
PDD at $106.23: market cap ~$150B. Zero buyback, zero dividend. BABA: $25B buyback committed. JD: $5B program. PDD has largest cash pile of any e-commerce company returning nothing.
FY2025 R&D: RMB 16,496.2M (+30% YoY). R&D/revenue rose to 3.8% from 3.2%. Focus: supply chain modernization, algorithm improvements.
RMB 100B merchant/farmer ecosystem program (3-year). Includes RMB 10B fee reductions, 'New Quality Supply', free delivery to villages. Two specific initiatives named in Q4 call.
Li Lu/Himalaya Capital: $482M position at ~$105, 14.6% of $3.57B portfolio, held through Q4. Tiger Global: 100% exit. Mirae: -99.1% (-$4.1B). Goldman: -41.9% (-$764M). Fidelity: -45.9% (-$1.33B).
Section 122 (15% global tariff) expires July 24, 2026. Treasury Sec. Bessent targets new Section 301 tariffs to restore pre-SCOTUS levels by August 2026. Key swing dates for H2 2026.
Alibaba FY2025: revenue +7%, AI/cloud +36%. Quick commerce EBITA -47% YoY. Cash $50.5B. $25B+ buyback committed through March 2027.
Douyin GMV ~RMB 3.5T (2024), targeting 4.2T (2025). Growth decelerating from 60%+ to ~30%. Shelf e-commerce now 50% of GMV. RMB 32B merchant subsidies.
Shein IPO: FCA approved but CSRC blocked. Valuation crashed from $100B to ~$30B. Net profit down 40% YoY. Amazon Haul: 25+ markets, 1M+ items, visits tripled.
